introSection: Parrot Coaster (Chinese: 鹦鹉过山车) is a steel winged roller coaster located at Chimelong Ocean Kingdom in Zhuhai, Guangdong, China. It was manufactured by Bolliger & Mabillard and is a Wing Coaster model. The ride is the first winged roller coaster in Asia. With a height of, it was also the second tallest winged roller coaster, after GateKeeper at Cedar Point.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: The track is painted dark green and the supports are a beige color.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content:
8 cars per train. Riders are arranged 4 across in a single row for a total of 32 riders per train.

The trains have a large parrot head in the middle as the theme is that riders are flying through a rainforest. The lead car from one of Flying Over the Rainforest's trains was on show at B&M's booth at the 2012 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

fullText:
Parrot Coaster (Chinese: 鹦鹉过山车) is a steel winged roller coaster located at Chimelong Ocean Kingdom in Zhuhai, Guangdong, China. It was manufactured by Bolliger & Mabillard and is a Wing Coaster model. The ride is the first winged roller coaster in Asia. With a height of, it was also the second tallest winged roller coaster, after GateKeeper at Cedar Point.



The track is painted dark green and the supports are a beige color.

8 cars per train. Riders are arranged 4 across in a single row for a total of 32 riders per train.

The trains have a large parrot head in the middle as the theme is that riders are flying through a rainforest. The lead car from one of Flying Over the Rainforest's trains was on show at B&M's booth at the 2012 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.
